title: MAP System Adherence Audit: verify the 11 new systems are fully-featured, cross-linked, and actually used
role: Claude, acting as Process Steward per PROMO-0007/IDEA-0012
description: Operator concern (hcom #22305): the 11 systems built in TASK-103-126 (Research, Self-Repair, Context, Decision/Authority, Human Interface, Risk, Security/Permissions, Change Control, Project Bootstrapping, Archive/Retention, Retrospective) plus core MAP infrastructure (HPOM, RnS/limit-watcher, Emergence/E-I, LangGraph orchestration) may have been built fast without verifying they are fully-featured against their own stated scope, genuinely cross-linked to each other (not just one-directional mentions), and actually being used in practice rather than just existing as documentation. This audit checks each system for: (1) completeness against its own acceptance criteria/core principle, (2) bidirectional cross-links verified by direct file inspection, (3) real usage evidence (grep for actual invocations/copies of templates, actual DEC entries using DECISION_CLASSES.md's Class field, actual context packets built, etc.) versus just existing. Findings are NOT silently fixed - each becomes a Repair Record (per SELF_REPAIR_SYSTEM.md) or a new task, routed through normal review/release. Promoted from IDEA-0012 (PROMO-0007) per direct operator instruction that this kind of check needs to happen and currently isn't.
acceptance_criteria: Every one of the 11 systems (Research through Retrospective) is checked against its own stated acceptance criteria/core principle for completeness
Cross-links between all 11 systems are verified bidirectional by direct file inspection, not assumed from memory
Real usage evidence is gathered for each system (grep-based, not just 'the doc exists') distinguishing genuinely-used from documented-but-unused
Findings are filed as Repair Records or new tasks per SELF_REPAIR_SYSTEM.md severity, not silently fixed in place
A single audit report exists under MAP_System/artifacts/audits/ summarizing all findings
